examples.title: Generate SSH Key Pair
examples.description: Command to generate Ed25519 SSH key pair
examples.code:
# Generate Ed25519 key pair (recommended)
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "user@hostname"

# Or generate RSA key
ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "user@hostname"

# Save with custom name
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/custom_key -C "user@hostname"
examples.explanation: ssh-keygen generates public/private key pair. -t specifies type, -b bits for RSA, -C adds comment, -f specifies file location

examples.title: SSH Connection and Port Forwarding
examples.description: Various SSH connection examples with port forwarding
examples.code:
# Basic SSH connection
ssh user@hostname

# SSH with specific port
ssh -p 2222 user@hostname

# Local port forwarding (-L)
# Forward local port 8080 to remote host's port 80
ssh -L 8080:remotehost:80 user@gateway

# Remote port forwarding (-R)
# Allow remote server to access local port 3000
ssh -R 3000:localhost:3000 user@remoteserver

# Dynamic port forwarding (SOCKS proxy)
ssh -D 9090 user@gateway
examples.explanation: -L creates local tunnel, -R creates remote tunnel, -D creates dynamic SOCKS proxy. These enable tunneling traffic through SSH

examples.title: Secure File Transfer Commands
examples.description: SCP and SFTP commands for secure file transfer
examples.code:
# SCP - Copy file to remote
scp file.txt user@hostname:/path/to/destination/

# SCP - Copy file from remote
scp user@hostname:/path/to/file.txt ./local/

# SCP - Copy directory recursively
scp -r folder/ user@hostname:/path/

# SFTP - Interactive file transfer
sftp user@hostname
# Then use: get, put, ls, cd, mkdir, pwd, exit

# Secure copy with preservation
scp -p file.txt user@hostname:/destination/
examples.explanation: SCP is command-line based file copy, SFTP provides interactive FTP-like interface. Both use SSH for encryption

examples.title: SSH Connection Flow
examples.code:
BEGIN
 // Client initiates connection
 client.connect(server, port=22)
 
 // Protocol version exchange
 version_exchange()
 
 // Key exchange (Diffie-Hellman)
 client_keys = generate_ephemeral_keys()
 server_keys = exchange(client_keys)
 session_key = derive_session_key(client_keys, server_keys)
 
 // Server authentication
 server_prove_identity(server_keys)
 client.verify(server_keys, known_hosts)
 
 // User authentication
 IF auth_method == "password" THEN
 client.send_encrypted(password)
 server.verify(password)
 ELSE IF auth_method == "public_key" THEN
 client.sign(data, private_key)
 server.verify(public_key, signature)
 END IF
 
 // Session established
 open_secure_channel(session_key)
 // Ready for commands
END
examples.explanation: Shows the SSH connection process: connection, key exchange, server authentication, user authentication, and session establishment
